Class Pattern

java.lang.Object
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.dvs.Pattern
public class Pattern extends Object
Pattern which defines the format of version IDs.

Each pattern is composed of segments, using whose elements IDs can be parsed. Learn more about version IDs, segments and patterns on the wiki.

Patterns are immutable - it is recommended to create individual objects only once and reuse them.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Pattern(Segment... segments)
    Creates a pattern composed of the given segments, ordered from the most significant to the least significant (left to right).

  • Constructor Details

    • Pattern

      public Pattern(@NotNull Segment... segments)
      Creates a pattern composed of the given segments, ordered from the most significant to the least significant (left to right).

      To avoid any parsing conflicts, make sure that no neighboring segments are overlapping. Here are the two common solutions to that:

      1. Reserve a character/sequence (e.g. ".") and create it's own segment: Segment.literal("."). Make sure the reserved sequence does not appear anywhere else. Then, separate each changing segment (range with 2+ numbers, literal segment with 2+ elements) with this segment, for example:
        new Pattern(Segment.range(0, Integer.MAX_VALUE), Segment.literal("."), Segment.range(0, 10))
        This way you will still be able to maintain great version ID semantics: 1.0 -> 1.1...
      2. Ensure all elements within all segments are of the same length in characters. For range segments, you can use filling.
      Parameters:
      segments - the segments, ordered from left (most-significant) to right (least-significant)

  • Method Details

    • getPart

      @Deprecated @NotNull public Segment getPart(int index)
      Deprecated.
      Method with confusing name, use getSegment(int) instead. Subject for removal.

    • getSegment

      @NotNull public Segment getSegment(int index)
      Returns segment at the given index (index 0 refers to the most significant one on the left).
      Parameters:
      index - the index
      Returns:
      segment at the given index

    • getVersion

      @Nullable public Version getVersion(@NotNull String versionId)
      Parses the given version ID. If the ID does not match this pattern, returns null.
      Parameters:
      versionId - the version ID to parse
      Returns:
      the version, or null if cannot parse

    • getFirstVersion

      public Version getFirstVersion()
      Builds and returns the first version specified by this pattern, being the version with all cursors set to 0.
      Returns:
      the first version
